display.purpose: Hero H1 only. Black (900), tight tracking, text-balance splits needed lines.

body.purpose: Paragraphs, 400 weight, line-length 65-75ch. 500/700 for inline emphasis.

label.purpose: Buttons, pills, names, UI. 700-800, sentence case.

caption.purpose: JetBrains Mono, dates/stat captions. The ONLY uppercase-tracked (0.15em) treatment; never a kicker over a title.

shadows.name: sm
shadows.purpose: Resting lift under the primary CTA and small chips. Barely there.

shadows.name: md
shadows.purpose: Hover state on cards, dropdowns, popovers.

shadows.name: tag-hard
shadows.purpose: HighlightTag signature: a hard offset in a darker tone of the fill, never black. A graphic device, not elevation.

motion.name: ease-pm
motion.purpose: The app-wide standard ease-out. No bounce, no elastic.

motion.name: duration-color
motion.purpose: Default color-transition duration on buttons, links, hovers.

motion.name: reveal
motion.purpose: Scroll-in reveal: fade + directional slide, optional blur. Staggered ~90ms between siblings.

motion.name: count-up
motion.purpose: Stats animate 0 to value on scroll into view; tabular-nums.

motion.name: marquee
motion.purpose: Seamless revolving strip; pauses on hover, edge-faded.

motion.name: reduced-motion
motion.purpose: All of the above are neutralised: content forced visible, numbers jump to final. Content is never stranded.

motion.name: glow-field
motion.value: 34rem blurred circle, bg-pm-blue-glow/25, cursor-follow with ~0.07 lerp easing
motion.purpose: Ambient ombre glow drifting after the cursor inside a Washed Voices Band. Transform-only. Rests centred on touch/(hover:none); off under prefers-reduced-motion.

components.name: Primary Button (the CTA)
components.description: Orange = action. At most one per screen, for the conversion. Blue focus ring = identity.

components.name: Outline Button (workhorse)
components.description: The default secondary action: bordered, surface fill, ink text.

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Text-only ink label; hover feedback is a subtle surface tint, never colored text.

components.name: Highlight Tag (signature)
components.description: The signature move: a tilted highlighter-marker label with a hard bottom shadow in a darker tone of its own fill. Always skewed (no flat option), ink text, sentence case, only above a left-aligned title.

components.name: Content Card
components.description: Surface panel defined by a 1px hairline at rest, not a shadow. Generous 28px corners; restrained lift on hover. Nested cards forbidden.
components.html:
Hands-on sessions, led by people who ship.

Real practitioners teaching the approaches they use every day.
components.css: .ds-card{background:#fff;color:#191919;border:1px solid #dcdde1;border-radius:28px;padding:28px;transition:transform .3s cubic-bezier(0.2,0,0,1),box-shadow .3s cubic-bezier(0.2,0,0,1),border-color .3s;}.ds-card:hover{transform:translateY(-6px);border-color:rgba(25,25,25,.2);box-shadow:0 12px 32px rgb(25 25 25/.08);}.ds-card-title{margin:0;font-family:Noto Sans Display, Noto Sans, system-ui, sans-serif;font-weight:900;font-size:1.5rem;line-height:1.15;letter-spacing:-0.01em;}.ds-card-body{margin:.75rem 0 0;font-family:Noto Sans, system-ui, sans-serif;font-weight:400;font-size:1rem;line-height:1.6;color:#3f3f46;}

components.name: Meta Pill / Chip
components.description: Small inset chip for dates, formats, counts. Ink-2 label on surface-2, hairline border, full radius. Categorical chips may take a highlighter-cast fill.

components.name: Input Field
components.description: Bordered surface field, 12px-family radius. Focus shifts the border to identity blue and adds a 3px blue ring.

narrative.overview: Product Makers is a well-typeset zine made by makers, rebuilt as a website. The register is brand: this is a community marketing site, so the design is the product. It runs on three physical-object words: hand-lettered, loud, document-like. Confident black display type on warm off-white paper, two loud brand colors used as paint (never decoration), and the signature move: tilted highlighter-marker labels that look drawn on by hand. It must feel made by a person, not generated. Structure is done with 1px ink-tinted rules, not shadows, so the page reads like a well-set document. Loudness is rationed: the blue drenched hero and the single orange CTA are the set-pieces; everything between them is quiet ink-on-paper so the loud moments land. The voice matches the look: welcoming, second-person, Slack-fluent, quietly confident, sentence case, with 1-to-3-word imperative buttons and free as the headline.
keyCharacteristics: Instantly recognizable: tilted highlighter labels with a hard bottom shadow, black Noto Sans Display on warm paper, blue #3f3fff identity + orange #ff5500 action, blue text-selection, borders instead of shadows.
Committed on brand set-pieces (blue drenched hero, orange CTA), Restrained on content (ink on paper, hairline rules).
One conversion per screen; Grow / Build / Connect is the routing spine.
Real faces and names over abstract graphics; the community is the product.
No dark mode (deliberately undefined), no script/cursive face, no gradients on chrome.

rules.name: The Two-Paint Rule
rules.body: Blue is identity, orange is action, and they never swap. Blue never fills a CTA; orange never fills anything but the one conversion button per screen.

rules.name: The No-Colored-Text Rule
rules.body: Text is ink (or its opacity steps) on light, white on dark. Blue, orange and the highlighter cast are paint (fills for buttons, tags, canvases, focus rings, the logo), never applied to text. Emphasis is weight and size; a link is weight + underline or an arrow, never a hue.

rules.name: The Rationed-Loud Rule
rules.body: Saturated color carries the hero and the CTA and stops there. On any content screen the loud colors sit near 10% of the surface; their rarity is what makes them read as loud.

rules.name: The Sentence-Case Rule
rules.body: Everything is sentence case: headings, buttons, labels, nav. No ALL-CAPS marketing shouts. The one exception is the mono spec caption.

rules.name: The Weight-Not-Color Rule
rules.body: Hierarchy comes from scale + weight (>=1.25 step ratio) and nothing else. Never lighten, tint, or color a word to rank it.

rules.name: The Titles-Use-Available-Width Rule
rules.body: A heading breaks to a second line only when it genuinely does not fit. Display titles get a wide cap (max-w-5xl). Left-aligned section leads use max-w-4xl so descriptions fill the width instead of cramming; centered leads may stay max-w-2xl.

rules.name: The Borders-Not-Shadows Rule
rules.body: Structure is drawn with 1px rules and tonal steps. If a layout needs a shadow to feel organized, the border and spacing are wrong first. Shadows appear only as a response to state (hover, float, focus), never at rest on a content card.

rules.name: The No-Glass Rule
rules.body: No glassmorphism, no backdrop-blur cards, no glow, no neon. Gradients live only inside sanctioned hero/canvas set-pieces, never on chrome, buttons, or content cards.

rules.name: The Watermark Rule
rules.body: The Makers script appears in backgrounds at ONE treatment only, implemented once in pm/script-watermark.tsx (never hand-roll a second): the BLACK script zoomed to 180% of the band width and centered, so the letterforms bleed off every edge and the word never reads in full. Opacity is a whisper that darkens the ground tone-on-tone: 8% on blue set-pieces (reads as deeper blue), 5% on light grounds (reads as soft grey). No blur, no rotation beyond the script natural slant, always aria-hidden and behind every content layer. The ground is always the brand wash: blue set-pieces run the 135deg blue-bright to blue to blue-press gradient (plus at most a soft glow orb); light grounds stay paper with a gentle blue-soft/lavender wash.

rules.name: The Washed Voices Band
rules.body: Every testimonial/quote carousel (a Marquee of quote cards) sits on a vertical wash, not a flat paper/surface-2 band: rises out of whatever surface precedes it, peaks at blue-soft behind the cards, and settles into whatever surface follows (bg-linear-to-b from-{prev} via-pm-blue-soft/70 to-{next}); a hero directly above hands off from full blue instead (from-pm-blue-soft). Layer in order: the light-tone ScriptWatermark, GlowField, then the content at relative. White quote cards pop off the wash; the Marquee edge-fade is a transparent mask so it never smears a wrong colour. Turns a hero-to-content or content-to-content seam from an abrupt cut into a held note.

dos: Do paint with the two brand colors in role: blue #3f3fff for identity (logo, focus rings, hero field), orange #ff5500 for the single action per screen.
Do open a section with its title, or with a left-aligned skewed HighlightTag, and nothing above it.
Do keep every HighlightTag clearly tilted (3 degrees minimum) with a hard bottom shadow in a darker tone of its own fill.
Do build structure with 1px #dcdde1 rules and tonal steps (paper to surface to surface-2); reach for a shadow only on hover/float/focus.
Do set everything in Noto Sans / Noto Sans Display, sentence case, hierarchy from weight + size.
Do write welcoming, second-person, Slack-fluent copy; buttons are 1 to 3 word imperatives; make free the headline.
Do show real faces, names and event photos; when a photo is missing use an initials avatar, never a fabricated person.
Do let titles use the available width and only wrap when they truly must; give leads max-w-4xl when left-aligned.
Do keep motion reduced-motion safe, eased-out, and off layout properties.
donts: Don't apply blue, orange, or any highlighter hue to text for emphasis or as a link color. No colored text, ever.
Don't put an eyebrow / kicker over a title (no small tracked uppercase label sitting above a heading).
Don't use a script or cursive font, or any face mimicking the Makers wordmark; the wordmark is an SVG image, never type.
Don't fill a CTA with blue or use orange for anything but the one conversion button per screen.
Don't stack a Highlighter marker-sweep and a HighlightTag on the same title, and don't center a tag.
Don't use em dashes in copy; use commas, colons, periods, or parentheses. Also not --.
Don't add decorative icons, step numbers, or random separators; only the Grow / Build / Connect set is sanctioned.
Don't ship the SaaS-landing template: cream background, Inter, gradient-text hero, the big-number stat-row template, or identical icon-heading-text card grids.
Don't use glassmorphism, glow, neon-on-black, side-stripe (border-left) accents, gradients on chrome, or nested cards.
Don't introduce dark mode or a purple/gradient AI-tool skin; if it could read 'AI made that,' rework it.
